titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 579

p.seg: Malcolm, king of the Scots, to the prior of Coldingham, greeting.

Know that I will and resolutely instruct that you fully have every liberty that I have confirmed to you by my charter, that is to say, that you have your wood and warren throughout your land and that in respect of your woods and your warren no one presume to intrude or to enter them unless by your permission, and if by chance anyone may have undertaken to violate this liberty, he shall do it on pain of my full forfeiture.
As witnesses: Walter the chancellor, David Oliphant.
